title: Golden Globes Analysis: Are Oscars Ready For One More Battle w/ Hamnet?
text: At the 2026 Golden Globes, the film Hamnet secured the Best Motion Picture Drama award, with star Jessie Buckley winning Lead Female Actor. Director Chloé Zhao celebrated the wins enthusiastically at the post-ceremony party. The article suggests Hamnet could pose a challenge to the current frontrunner One Battle After Another at the Oscars. Critic Pete Hammond notes that emotional films often resonate strongly with Academy voters, despite Hamnet's modest box office performance thus far. Having producer Steven Spielberg present the award carries significant weight with Oscar voters. Hammond observes that many viewers may not have heard of the movie but Spielberg's endorsement matters considerably. Crucially, the Oscars use preferential voting for Best Picture, meaning second and third-place choices can prove decisive. This system historically benefits emotionally resonant films like Hamnet, similar to how Moonlight defeated La La Land in 2017. While One Battle After Another remains the overwhelming frontrunner, winning consistently across award ceremonies, Hamnet's passionate supporters and emotional impact position it as a potential underdog contender heading into Oscar nominations on January 22.
